FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Sony gets OCC nod for stablecoin trust charter, Goldman lands $70B in asset management wins, and Fed divisions on rates could extend the easing debate.

bullets.text: Sony receives conditional OCC approval to launch Connectia Trust, a stablecoin issuer and manager, by 2027—marking a major fintech entrant into regulated banking.
Goldman Sachs secures $70B in asset management mandates from Verizon and Lockheed Martin, muscling deeper into corporate retirement plans against BlackRock and Mercer.
Fed FOMC minutes will expose ongoing rate-cut divisions between hawks and doves; internal squabbling could stretch the easing debate across multiple meetings.
Activist investors targeting United Bancorporation of Alabama to deploy $123M in equity through M&A, buybacks, or dividends after the bank let expenses balloon and deposits stagnate.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Asset & wealth management stories dominated by RIA consolidation, aggregation sector headwinds, and Fisher Investments' contrarian organic-growth strategy.

bullets.text: Morningstar's ByAllAccounts sale to Pello is failing to close—another blow to the fragile account aggregation sector as Plaid preps for IPO and CFPB weighs permanent data-fee rules.
Fisher Investments grew AUM nearly 4x ($112B to ~$450B) since 2019 via organic growth and media dominance, spurning M&A while competitors consolidate.
RIA consolidation continues: Wavvest snagged a $1.4B Schwab advisor; Choreo acquired a $700M planning-focused firm under new CEO Van de Loo.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: 5 stories spanning M&A approvals, supply chain insurance expansion, geopolitical risk, and regulatory shifts in pricing frameworks.

bullets.text: Amwins/Dragoneer extend exclusivity on $5.3B Steadfast bid as deal moves toward shareholder vote—Australian insurance consolidation advancing.
European Commission clears Zurich's Beazley acquisition under simplified review with no competition concerns—cross-border deal cleared.
Hormuz shipping standoff after US-Iran strikes creates marine insurance exposure spike and energy volatility—geopolitical tail risk activating.
California AB 311 would allow telematics discounts in auto insurance, potentially upending Prop 103 rate-setting restrictions—regulatory thaw brewing.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: 5 stories spanning data center capital rotation, record credit fundraising, and strategic specialty finance M&A amid infrastructure buildout tailwinds.

bullets.text: Equifax closes $750M acquisition of Mexico's Círculo de Crédito, betting on high-growth emerging market credit bureau consolidation.
17 banks across 6 countries piloting live tokenized deposits on Swift's blockchain ledger—targeting 24/7 settlement and unlocking liquidity efficiency gains.
Credit fundraising hits record H1 2026 levels; PDI forecasting potential full-year record if momentum sustains despite macro headlines.
Data center real estate reshaping CMBS capital allocation—traditional CRE losing share to power-intensive AI infrastructure spec financing.

PE & Deals.lede: PE fundraising hits records driven by AI mega-funds; Russell Investments sale signals institutional consolidation; capital concentration squeezing mid-market deals.

bullets.text: B Capital consortium acquiring Russell Investments—a major institutional index/allocation platform—underscoring PE appetite for fintech infrastructure assets.
MGX's $49bn fund close on AI thesis shows mega-fund fundraising now driven primarily by AI exposure; LP demand overriding traditional diversification logic.
Mid-market PE M&A contracting as capital concentrates at top tier; GP-led secondaries projected to triple, signaling portfolio recycling as primary deal driver.
Temasek rebalancing EMEA allocations toward AI, private credit, and infrastructure by 2031 after acknowledging direct investment underperformance in the region.

Markets & Macro.lede: European banks weaponizing synthetic risk transfers to skirt capital rules; PE sponsors hunting exits; Fed rate pause under fire.

bullets.text: European banks aggressively deploying synthetic risk transfers (SRTs) to shed loan risk off-book and reduce regulatory capital without shrinking portfolios—accelerating as ECB stress tests tighten and deposit costs rise.
Blackstone and TPG hunting >$4bn buyer for Hologic minority stake or full exit—18 months after $6.2bn debt-heavy acquisition, signaling pressure to de-lever and return capital from the LBO.
Fed's rate-hold stance questioned as structurally unjustified given labor market strength and inflation backdrop—op-ed argues data doesn't support continued pause.

Newsletters.lede: 5 stories spanning payment networks, wealth management pivots, SEC advisor rules, single-stock ETFs, and a Treasury win for State Street.

bullets.text: Banks exploring card network acquisitions to dodge interchange fee caps that have cost issuers tens of billions—regulatory arbitrage in action.
Wealth managers shifting direct-indexing SMAs toward long-short strategies as rising markets erode tax-loss harvesting edge; fee pressure forcing product innovation.
SEC proposal to auto-grant accredited status to advised clients creates compliance complexity; advisor community split on access vs. risk.
State Street's SPYM selected as core holding for Trump administration accounts, signaling Treasury's preference amid broader single-stock ETF filing surge.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: AI economics shift toward data quality and cost efficiency; meanwhile, infrastructure ambition and governance frameworks emerge as parallel concerns.

bullets.text: Data—not compute—is now the competitive moat in AI: Meta, xAI, and Palantir are winning on access to high-quality, verifiable datasets rather than model size alone.
Grok 4.5 delivers frontier performance at 10x lower cost through intelligent routing; the routing layer pays for itself in a week, signaling a major repricing of AI labor.
AI governance is becoming operationally critical for financial institutions, but constitutional frameworks like Claude's Constitution raise unresolved questions about legitimacy and consent.

all.lede: M&A momentum across fintech and financial services: Amwins/Dragoneer push Steadfast deal forward, B Capital eyes Russell Investments, and Equifax expands into Mexico.

bullets.text: Amwins and Dragoneer reaffirm A$7.7B ($5.34B) bid for Steadfast Group with exclusivity extended four weeks as deal heads toward shareholder vote.
B Capital-led consortium including CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments, a major institutional asset-allocation platform, in significant fintech consolidation.
Equifax buying Círculo de Crédito for $750M to gain foothold in Mexico's fast-growing credit bureau market.
Wall Street banks exploring acquisition of card payment network to sidestep interchange fee caps that have cost issuers tens of billions.
